channel.title: đźŞ´ Zero's Garden
channel.description: Last 10 notes on đźŞ´ Zero's Garden

item.title: tree-sitter
item.description: Use existing *-ts-mode package Â§ First, install packages in package.el. (package! tree-sitter-langs :pin "3a3ad0527d5f8c7768678878eb5cfe399bedf703") (package! typst-ts-mode :recipe (:host sourcehut :repo "l2dy/typst-ts-mode") :pin "39a9e63c019bd5498c8f0e5a7ee8cb9d6bb53fd0") If package depends on treesit instead of tree-sitter, itâ€™s required to copy and rename parser library files.

item.title: Emacs Features
item.description: Put a call to provide at the end of each separate Lisp file. provide and require are an alternative to autoload for loading files automatically. They work in terms of named features.

item.title: autoload
item.description: Magic autoload comment Â§ A magic autoload comment (often called an autoload cookie) consists of ;;;###autoload, on a line by itself, just before the real definition of the function in its autoload-able source file.

item.title: AWS Map
item.description: Networking Â§ The AWS Backbone Network connects AWS Regions, Availability Zones, and the Internet. VPC is a SDN built for AWS powering isolated virtual networks.

item.title: lispy
item.description: Key Bindings Â§ Slurp: > Barf: < Clone: c Forward: ] Backward: [ Jump to matching pair: d Split: M-j Join: + Insert Â§ Parens: ( Braces: { Brackets: } Quotes: " LispyVille Â§ As described in https://github.

item.title: tty
item.description: +osc Â§ In Mosh, SSH_TTY is left as is and points to a dead TTY. unset SSH_TTY in .zprofile or read tty from tmux display-message -p '#{pane_tty}' in a nested tmux session.

item.title: starship
item.description: Configuration Â§ Set format to skip slow modules, instead of disabling them individually. For example, Iâ€™m using # ~/.config/starship.toml format = '$username$hostname$directory$git_branch$git_commit$git_state$git_metrics$git_status$hg_branch$character'.

item.title: Zsh
item.description: New User Setup Â§ You should not edit anything between these lines if you intend to run zsh-newuser-install again. You may, however, edit any other part of the file.

item.title: Zero's Garden
item.description: Welcome to my digital garden, where I keep notes of my thoughts and knowledge about topics Iâ€™m interested in like computer science. Notes are loosely grouped into topics and interconnected, and there is no strict hierarchy.
